title: Atomic Habits
author: James Clear
description: A practical and science-backed guide to building good habits, breaking bad ones, and making tiny behavioral changes that lead to remarkable long-term personal and professional improvement.

title: The Alchemist
author: Paulo Coelho
description: A philosophical and inspirational story about a young shepherd's journey to discover his destiny, follow his dreams, and understand the deeper meaning of life and personal legend.

title: Sapiens
author: Yuval Noah Harari
description: A thought-provoking exploration of human history, tracing the evolution of Homo sapiens from ancient ancestors to modern civilization, focusing on culture, religion, and technological development.

title: Clean Code
author: Robert C. Martin
description: A foundational handbook for software developers that teaches principles of writing clean, readable, and maintainable code using best practices in agile software craftsmanship.

title: Rich Dad Poor Dad
author: Robert Kiyosaki
description: A personal finance classic that contrasts two financial mindsets and teaches essential lessons about money, investing, financial independence, and wealth building strategies.

title: Brief Answers to the Big Questions
author: Stephen Hawking
description: A collection of profound insights addressing the universe's biggest mysteries, including black holes, time travel, artificial intelligence, and the future of humanity.

title: Think and Grow Rich
author: Napoleon Hill
description: A timeless self-help book that explores the principles of success, mindset, and personal achievement based on studying the habits of wealthy and influential individuals.

title: The Hobbit
author: J.R.R. Tolkien
description: A classic fantasy adventure following Bilbo Baggins as he embarks on an unexpected journey filled with dragons, treasure, and epic encounters in Middle-earth.

title: A Brief History of Time
author: Stephen Hawking
description: An accessible yet deep exploration of cosmology, covering black holes, the Big Bang, space-time, and the fundamental nature of the universe.

title: The Psychology of Money
author: Morgan Housel
description: A collection of insightful lessons about how people think about money, wealth, greed, and happiness, focusing on behavior rather than mathematics.

title: Harry Potter and the Sorcerer's Stone
author: J.K. Rowling
description: The beginning of a magical series that follows Harry Potter as he discovers his wizarding identity and enters a world of spells, friendship, and dark secrets.

title: The Selfish Gene
author: Richard Dawkins
description: A groundbreaking work in evolutionary biology explaining how genes drive evolution and behavior, shifting focus from organisms to genetic survival strategies.
